title: Scriptwriter-Inspired Creativity Optimization Algorithm
author: Zhang, Jincheng
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Screenwriters play a crucial role in the creation of film, television, stage plays, and online literature. Their core mission is to express plot, characters, dialogue, and thematic ideas through text. Traditional screenwriting relies on creativity and experience, lacking systematic quantitative methods. With the development of computer-assisted creation, it has become possible to transform screenwriters' creative characteristics into mathematical models and optimization algorithms. This paper proposes a Scriptwriter-Inspired Creativity Optimization (SICO) algorithm. This algorithm combines inspiration triggering mechanisms, emotional wave functions, and a character interaction matrix to optimize script structure across three dimensions: scene selection, emotional flow, and character interaction, reflecting the unique characteristics of screenwriting. The algorithm describes the creative inspiration process through mathematical formulas and provides a quantifiable method for scene ranking. This method not only simulates screenwriters' creative inspiration but also optimizes script quality across multiple dimensions, providing a theoretical foundation for AI-assisted creation.
